memes.label: Adolescence
memes.description: The transitional period between childhood and adulthood that shapes the central characters of The Counterfeiters, marked by moral awakening, self-discovery, and the tension between innocence and experience

memes.label: Albéric Profitendieu
memes.description: "Examining magistrate and the man Bernard believed to be his father, whose discovery of the truth about his wife's affair and Bernard's flight from home opens the novel's exploration of illegitimacy, bourgeois respectability, and the fragility of familial bonds"

memes.label: André Gide
memes.description: The author of The Counterfeiters (Les Faux-monnayeurs, 1925), whose aesthetic theories and moral philosophy permeate the novel through the character of Edouard and the novel's experimental form

memes.label: Angel
memes.description: A symbolic figure in The Counterfeiters representing childhood innocence, purity, and the visible manifestation of goodness — associated with the children Bronja and Boris

memes.label: Armand Vedel
memes.description: Olivier's cynical and intelligent friend, a pastor's son who rejects his Protestant upbringing and masks his sensitivity with deliberate vulgarity, declaring "I'm a Catholic" as an act of defiance

memes.label: "Armand's Rebellion"
memes.description: "Armand Vedel declares his cynical rejection of Protestant piety at Laura's wedding, asserting his Catholicism as a gesture of rebellion"

memes.label: Art
memes.description: The novel's self-reflexive concern with the nature and purpose of art — what makes art genuine or counterfeit, and whether art serves life or substitutes for it

memes.label: Azaïs
memes.description: The elderly pastor who heads the Vedel-Azaïs school, whose rigid moral austerity provides a veneer of respectability over an institution that becomes the setting for the novel's darkest events

memes.label: Azaïs Pension
memes.description: The Protestant boarding school run by the Vedel-Azaïs family near the Luxembourg Gardens — a microcosm of bourgeois educational ideals, generational conflict, and the circulation of counterfeit values among the young

memes.label: Bernard and Olivier's Reunion
memes.description: "Bernard Profitendieu, having just discovered his illegitimacy and fled home, seeks out his schoolfriend Olivier Molinier in the Luxembourg Gardens — an encounter that initiates the central friendship of the novel and sets both characters on intertwined trajectories"

memes.label: Bernard and Sarah
memes.description: "Bernard's brief romantic encounter with Sarah Vedel at the Azaïs school — a fleeting liaison that belongs to the novel's pattern of provisional and experimental attachments"

memes.label: Bernard at the Pension
memes.description: "Bernard Profitendieu's residence at the Vedel-Azaïs boarding school — his immersion in the domestic atmosphere of the Vedel family and his gradually deepening curiosity about its members"

memes.label: Bernard Becomes Edouard's Secretary
memes.description: "Bernard Profitendieu, having fled his family home, offers himself as Edouard's secretary — a role that becomes the structural device for his moral education and gradual transformation across the novel"

memes.label: "Bernard Discovers His Illegitimacy"
memes.description: The inciting event of The Counterfeiters — Bernard Profitendieu finds his mother's old love letters and learns he is not the son of the man who raised him, setting in motion the novel's entire plot and its central themes of authenticity, self-invention, and counterfeit identity

memes.label: "Bernard Leaves Home"
memes.description: "Bernard writes his farewell letter to the magistrate and departs the Profitendieu household"

memes.label: "Bernard Meets Laura"
memes.description: "Bernard meets Laura Douviers at Saas-Fée, having already read her story in Edouard's stolen journal"

memes.label: Bernard Profitendieu
memes.description: One of the central young protagonists of The Counterfeiters — a schoolboy who discovers he is illegitimate, leaves home, reads Edouard's journal, and embarks on a journey of moral self-discovery that embodies the novel's ethic of authenticity

memes.label: Bernard's Return to Profitendieu
memes.description: "At the novel's close, Bernard returns to the Profitendieu household — a homecoming that represents not defeat but a hard-won recognition of the value of ordinary life, completing the arc from rebellion to reconciliation"

memes.label: Boris
memes.description: A young boy at the Swiss sanatorium who worries he will "always be naughty" and thus cannot see angels — a figure of childhood anxiety about moral purity whose fragility ends in tragedy

memes.label: "Boris at the Sanatorium"
memes.description: "At the Swiss sanatorium, Boris and Bronja discuss angels and purity, their childlike mysticism set against the adult world's corruption"

memes.label: Bourgeoisie
memes.description: The French Protestant middle class — its family structure, moral conventions, and social respectability — as the primary social target of Gide's The Counterfeiters, embodying the central counterfeit between public virtue and private reality

memes.label: Bronja
memes.description: Boris's Polish companion at the Swiss sanatorium, an "exquisite" girl who sees angels because she is not naughty, and whose death deepens Boris's despair to its fatal conclusion

memes.label: All Entries by Quality
memes.description: Complete listing of all wiki entries grouped by quality tier — featured, standard, basic.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: Index of all characters in André Gide's The Counterfeiters, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Childhood
memes.description: A thematic concern in The Counterfeiters — the vulnerability of children to adult corruption, the loss of innocence, and the tension between natural freedom and institutional constraint

memes.label: Coming of Age
memes.description: The narrative arc of moral and psychological maturation in The Counterfeiters, marked by disillusionment, self-discovery, and the recognition that adulthood is not arrival but ongoing questioning

memes.label: Comte de Passavant
memes.description: A commercially successful but aesthetically and morally hollow novelist — Edouard's rival and the novel's embodiment of counterfeit art and worldly corruption

memes.label: Concepts & Themes
memes.description: Index of literary and philosophical concepts explored in André Gide's The Counterfeiters.

memes.label: Confession
memes.description: A narrative and thematic device in The Counterfeiters — the act of revealing hidden truths, central to character development and the novel's exploration of sincerity and self-knowledge

memes.label: Convalescence
memes.description: The state of recovery from illness or injury, a recurring motif in The Counterfeiters that serves as a period of physical vulnerability, moral reckoning, and potential transformation.

memes.label: Counterfeit
memes.description: The novel's titular concept, encompassing literal false coins, the inauthentic performance of identity, and the broader question of what constitutes genuine value in art, morality, and human relationships.

memes.label: Counterfeit Coin
memes.description: Central metaphor in André Gide's The Counterfeiters — the false gold coin that circulates as real, symbolizing inauthenticity in human relationships, art, and selfhood; the novel's title image and organizing principle

memes.label: Cowardice
memes.description: The failure of moral nerve — whether in action, speech, or self-knowledge — as a recurring pattern of conduct in The Counterfeiters

memes.label: Delinquency
memes.description: Adolescent transgression — from petty criminality to moral violation — as a central plot mechanism and thematic concern in The Counterfeiters

memes.label: The Devil
memes.description: A recurring figure in The Counterfeiters representing the force of temptation and rationalization — the devil "casts up his accounts" and provides moral beings with self-justifying reasons for transgression

memes.label: Edouard
memes.description: The central character of The Counterfeiters — a novelist writing a novel also called The Counterfeiters, whose journal structures the narrative, whose relationships with Olivier, Bernard, and Laura drive the plot, and whose artistic theories articulate the novel's aesthetic program

memes.label: Edouard Confronts Passavant
memes.description: "Edouard's visit to the Comte de Passavant to retrieve Olivier's belongings after Olivier's breakdown at the Argonauts banquet — a confrontation between the novel's two rival mentors, conducted in a register of icy politeness that masks profound antagonism"

memes.label: "Edouard Loses His Journal"
memes.description: "Edouard misplaces his suitcase containing his journal, which Bernard Profitendieu finds and reads at the station"

memes.label: "Edouard Visits La Pérouse"
memes.description: "Edouard visits the aged music-master at his entresol apartment in the Faubourg St. Honoré, where a life of disappointed hopes is revealed"

memes.label: Edouard's Reading of His Novel
memes.description: "At Saas-Fée, Edouard reads excerpts from his novel-in-progress to Bernard and Laura — a meta-fictional episode in which Gide dramatizes his own aesthetic theories through the device of a novelist explaining his work"

memes.label: Edouard's Studio
memes.description: Edouard's apartment and workspace in Paris, where Bernard works as his secretary and where key conversations, nocturnal crises, and moments of artistic and personal revelation take place

memes.label: Education
memes.description: The theme of upbringing and moral formation in The Counterfeiters — the failure of conventional education to shape character and the alternative educations offered by experience and self-discovery

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Index of key narrative events in André Gide's The Counterfeiters.

memes.label: Examination
memes.description: The institutional gateway between childhood and adulthood in The Counterfeiters — academic examinations, professional qualifications, and the broader metaphor of life as a test of character

memes.label: Family
memes.description: The institution of family as a source of constraint, secrecy, and identity in André Gide's The Counterfeiters

memes.label: Fashion
memes.description: The cultivation of personal style and surface appearance as a form of social performance, treated by Gide as both a vehicle for self-creation and a sign of moral superficiality.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: A central aspiration in The Counterfeiters — the ideal of personal liberty that characters pursue, fear, and sometimes achieve, set against the constraints of family, religion, and social convention

memes.label: Friedrich Nietzsche
memes.description: The German philosopher whose concepts are explicitly invoked in The Counterfeiters as an aesthetic model for the novel's formal innovation

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: The intimate bond between young men in The Counterfeiters, explored as a site of emotional authenticity, unspoken desire, and moral testing against the backdrop of bourgeois convention

memes.label: Fyodor Dostoevsky
memes.description: The Russian novelist whose psychological depth and moral complexity influenced Gide's conception of The Counterfeiters and whose narrative techniques inform the novel's polyphonic structure

memes.label: George Molinier
memes.description: The youngest Molinier son, a thirteen-year-old schoolboy involved in the counterfeit coin ring, whose petty theft and defiant attitude toward his uncle Edouard reveal the moral drift of the younger generation

memes.label: Georges's Expulsion
memes.description: "After Boris's suicide, Pauline Molinier withdraws her son George from the Pension Azaïs — an expulsion that is also a reckoning with the moral complicity George bears in the tragedy"
aliases: Pauline removes George from the pension

memes.label: Hamlet
memes.description: Shakespeare's tragedy, referenced by Bernard Profitendieu at the moment of his self-discovery — Hamlet's ghostly revelation of hidden truth becomes a model for Bernard's own discovery of his illegitimacy and his decision to reconstruct his identity

memes.label: Featured & Premium Pages
memes.description: Curated selection of the highest-quality pages in The Counterfeiters wiki — featured and premium entries.

memes.label: Honesty
memes.description: Bernard's declared highest virtue in The Counterfeiters — the quality of "ringing true" like a pure coin, contrasted with the counterfeit values that circulate through society

memes.label: Hospitality
memes.description: The social practice of receiving and sheltering others as it functions in The Counterfeiters — often as a screen for surveillance, hypocrisy, or uneasy obligation

memes.label: Illegitimacy
memes.description: In The Counterfeiters, illegitimacy extends beyond birth status to a philosophical principle — the condition of the natural child as emblem of authenticity, freedom, and the right to self-invention

memes.label: Illness
memes.description: Physical and nervous illness as a recurring motif in The Counterfeiters, where sickness serves as both a literal condition affecting characters and a metaphor for moral, psychological, and social disorder

memes.label: Intellectual Life
memes.description: The world of ideas, literature, and scholarly ambition that surrounds the characters of The Counterfeiters — a domain of liberation and entrapment in equal measure

memes.label: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
memes.description: The Genevan philosopher whose ideas about natural innocence, education, and the corrupting influence of society provide an intellectual counterpoint to The Counterfeiters' treatment of childhood and authenticity

memes.label: Journal (Edouard's Journal)
memes.description: The character Edouard's personal journal in The Counterfeiters — both a narrative device that structures the novel and a meditation on self-reflection, writing, and the relationship between lived experience and its record

memes.label: "Fables (La Fontaine)"
memes.description: Jean de La Fontaine's 17th-century verse fables, discussed during Bernard's baccalaureate examination — the self-portrait quality of La Fontaine's verse becomes the flashpoint for the novel's central debate about artistic sincerity, superficiality, and the French intellectual tradition

memes.label: La Pérouse
memes.description: The aged, impoverished music-master whose tragic story of his estranged son is one of the novel's most poignant episodes, and whose dignity in suffering provides a counterpoint to the young characters' restless dissatisfaction

memes.label: La Pérouse's Apartment
memes.description: The aged music-master's entresol apartment in the Faubourg St. Honoré, where he gives music lessons and confesses his life's tragedy to Edouard in a setting of faded gentility and bitter domestic strife

memes.label: "La Pérouse's Confession"
memes.description: "The aged music-master La Pérouse confesses to Edouard the tragic story of his son, who died young, and reveals the depth of his marriage's unhappiness"

memes.label: Lady Griffith (Lilian)
memes.description: A wealthy, amoral American woman who seduces Vincent Molinier, embodying a predatory charm that Edouard describes as having "no soul" — a figure of worldly counterfeit

memes.label: Laura Douviers
memes.description: A married woman in crisis — Vincent's abandoned mistress and Edouard's former intimate, whose suffering catalyzes the novel's central moral and emotional dramas

memes.label: "Laura's Letter to Edouard"
memes.description: "Edouard receives Laura Douviers's desperate letter and abandons his writing to return to Paris and help her"

memes.label: "Laura's Wedding"
memes.description: "Laura Douviers marries Félix Douviers in a modest ceremony at the chapel in Rue Madame, attended by the Vedel-Azaïs families"

memes.label: Le Havre
memes.description: The French port city on the Normandy coast where Edouard arrives from England at the start of his journey — the novel's threshold between the English and French worlds, and the point of entry that initiates the novel's central epistolary and geographic trajectories

memes.label: "L'Éducation Sentimentale"
memes.description: Gustave Flaubert's 1869 novel, referenced by Edouard as a model for his own notebook method and appearing as an epigraph to Part III — a key intertextual touchstone for The Counterfeiters's treatment of youth, disillusionment, and the novel of apprenticeship

memes.label: Literary Careerism
memes.description: The pursuit of literary fame and professional advancement through strategic social positioning, embodied most fully by Robert de Passavant and satirized through the world of Parisian literary journals.

memes.label: Luxembourg Gardens
memes.description: The public gardens in the 6th arrondissement of Paris where the novel's schoolboys gather after classes, the site of Bernard's first scene and the novel's opening social world

memes.label: Madame Profitendieu
memes.description: The wife of examining magistrate Albéric Profitendieu, whose past infidelity produced Bernard, and whose silent presence in the household represents the unspoken truths that structure the Profitendieu family

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: Marriage as social institution, emotional trap, and form of counterfeit in André Gide's The Counterfeiters — a public performance that masks private reality

memes.label: Mise en Abyme
memes.description: Gide's term from heraldry for a work of art that contains a smaller copy of itself — the novel-within-the-novel structure in The Counterfeiters, where Edouard writes a novel also called The Counterfeiters, creating a self-reflexive modernist form that mirrors the book's central theme of counterfeiting

memes.label: The Molinier Household
memes.description: The home of Pauline and Oscar Molinier near the Luxembourg Gardens, where Vincent, Olivier, and George grow up — the setting of maternal vigilance, paternal absence, and the generational dynamics that produce the novel's three Molinier sons

memes.label: Monsieur Molinier
memes.description: The father of Olivier, George, and Vincent Molinier, whose paternal authority and bourgeois respectability form the background against which his sons' rebellions take shape

memes.label: Moral (Moral Tragedy)
memes.description: Gide's conception of a specifically moral form of tragedy in The Counterfeiters — the drama of the soul's struggle with itself, distinct from the tragedy of fate, passion, or social circumstance

memes.label: Moral Choice
memes.description: The act of ethical decision-making under conditions of uncertainty and self-deception in The Counterfeiters, where characters must choose not between clear right and wrong but between competing goods, authentic and counterfeit values

memes.label: Moral Disorientation
memes.description: The erosion of traditional moral certainties that pervades the social and psychological landscape of The Counterfeiters

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: Top 30 most-referenced pages across André Gide's The Counterfeiters wiki, by total cross-references.

memes.label: Motherhood
memes.description: The maternal figures of The Counterfeiters — absent, complicit, or overbearing — whose relationships to their children shape the novel's central conflicts of identity and freedom

memes.label: Music
memes.description: A recurring motif in The Counterfeiters, associated with the character La Pérouse and with the tension between emotional expression and intellectual control

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: The natural world as a model for understanding human development in The Counterfeiters — Edouard's horticultural metaphor and the novel's broader investment in organic growth over artificial imposition

memes.label: The Novelist
memes.description: The figure of the novelist in Andre Gide's The Counterfeiters — both as a character type embodied by Edouard and as a vehicle for Gide's meta-fictional examination of the art of the novel

memes.label: Old Age
memes.description: The condition of elderly characters in The Counterfeiters — La Pérouse, Azaïs, and others — who confront the meaning of their past lives, the failure of their values, and the approach of death in a novel otherwise dominated by youth

memes.label: Olivier Meets Passavant
memes.description: "Olivier Molinier's first visit to the Comte de Passavant — the beginning of a fateful mentorship that will draw Olivier into the orbit of literary ambition, worldly sophistication, and moral compromise"
aliases: Olivier visits the Comte de Passavant

memes.label: Olivier Molinier
memes.description: Bernard's closest friend and Edouard's beloved nephew — a sensitive, intellectually precocious schoolboy caught between rival mentors and struggling with his own nature

memes.label: Organizations & Institutions
memes.description: Organizations, schools, and institutions that appear in André Gide's The Counterfeiters.

memes.label: Oscar Molinier
memes.description: The paternal grandfather of the Molinier brothers, whose name and legacy are part of the family background that the younger generation navigates

memes.label: Oscar Wilde
memes.description: The Irish writer whose aestheticism and personal fate resonated with Gide, and whose ideas about art's independence from morality inform The Counterfeiters' treatment of the relationship between aesthetic and ethical values

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The primary urban setting of The Counterfeiters (Parts I and III) — the city of bourgeois families, schoolboys, artists, and intellectuals whose social order the novel anatomizes as a system of counterfeit values, and the geographic center from which characters flee and to which they must return

memes.label: Pensées (Pascal)
memes.description: Blaise Pascal's posthumous philosophical fragments (1670), a cornerstone of French Catholic thought — quoted by Armand Vedel in his meditation on existential limits and used as the epigraph to Olivier's convalescence

memes.label: Passavant's Salon
memes.description: The fashionable literary salon where the Comte de Passavant holds court, cultivates his reputation as a man of letters, and draws Olivier and other young writers into his orbit of worldly success and aesthetic superficiality

memes.label: Pastor Vedel
memes.description: The old Protestant pastor, grandfather to Laura, Sarah, and Armand, whose pious rhetoric masks a failure to provide genuine moral education, and whose private diary reveals the gap between his public declarations and private struggles

memes.label: Pauline Molinier
memes.description: The half-sister of Edouard, wife of Monsieur Molinier, and mother of Olivier, George, and Vincent — the maternal presence who holds the Molinier household together amid her sons' various crises

memes.label: Places & Settings
memes.description: Index of locations and settings in André Gide's The Counterfeiters.

memes.label: Profitendieu
memes.description: The examining magistrate, Bernard's adoptive father, who investigates the counterfeit coin ring while grappling with the revelation that he is not Bernard's biological father

memes.label: The Profitendieu Household
memes.description: The bourgeois family home near the Luxembourg Gardens where Bernard Profitendieu grows up and from which he flees at the novel's opening — the archetypal domestic setting of family order, paternal authority, and filial rebellion

memes.label: "Profitendieu's Investigation"
memes.description: "The examining magistrate Albéric Profitendieu investigates the counterfeit coin ring, uncovering a network that extends from schoolboys to criminal suppliers"

memes.label: Protestantism
memes.description: The Protestant religious and cultural tradition that shapes the moral atmosphere of The Counterfeiters — its rigour, self-scrutiny, and Puritan inheritance — serving as both the psychological foundation of characters like Edouard, Laura, and the Vedel-Azais family, and the constraint against which the younger generation rebels

memes.label: Psychoanalysis
memes.description: The therapeutic and interpretive practice that enters The Counterfeiters through the character of Sophroniska, a Polish doctor who treats Boris's nervous disorders using dream analysis and confession — and whose methods Gide both employs and questions

memes.label: Pur (The Pure Novel)
memes.description: Edouard's aesthetic ideal in The Counterfeiters — a novel stripped of all elements not essential to the genre, parallel to Gide's own modernist project of purifying the novel form

memes.label: Rebellion
memes.description: A defining impulse in The Counterfeiters — the drive to resist authority, convention, and inherited morality, embodied in characters who "rebel" against their circumstances and the social order

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: The Protestant-Catholic tension in The Counterfeiters — a world shaped by Protestant moral seriousness and Catholic aesthetic difference, with characters navigating between inherited faith and modern unbelief

memes.label: Retreat
memes.description: A pattern of withdrawal from social confrontation or moral challenge, manifested by multiple characters in The Counterfeiters as both a survival strategy and a mark of weakness.

memes.label: Revolt
memes.description: A recurring moral and psychological phenomenon in The Counterfeiters — the instinct to rebel against family, convention, and domestic virtue, embodied particularly in the younger generation

memes.label: Robert de Passavant
memes.description: A wealthy, fashionable literary figure — the novel's major antagonist who runs a review, seduces and exploits young men (particularly Olivier Molinier), and represents counterfeit values in the literary world

memes.label: Rue Madame Chapel
memes.description: The small Protestant chapel on the Rue Madame in the 6th arrondissement where Laura Vedel's wedding takes place — a site of Protestant ritual and the novel's most concentrated depiction of religious domestic life

memes.label: Saas-Fée
memes.description: The Swiss mountain village in the Valais Alps where the sanatorium scenes of Part II take place — a space of alpine retreat, reflection, and moral confrontation that serves as the novel's geographic antithesis to Paris and the setting for its most concentrated philosophical exchanges

memes.label: Sarah Vedel
memes.description: Laura's younger sister, whose "instinct for revolt" is exasperated by family constraint, and whose provocative intimacy with Olivier at the wedding reception shocks Edouard

memes.label: Secrets
memes.description: The hidden knowledge that structures both plot and psychology in The Counterfeiters — the driving force of concealment, revelation, and the moral question of whether truth should always be told

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: A pervasive emotional force in The Counterfeiters, shaping characters' concealment of their true selves, moral struggles, and the tension between public respectability and private desire

memes.label: Sincerity
memes.description: A central ethical and aesthetic problem in The Counterfeiters — the impossibility of consistent sincerity in a self that is constantly changing; the novel's core moral question alongside the counterfeit coin metaphor

memes.label: Solitude
memes.description: The condition of being alone — chosen or imposed — that pervades The Counterfeiters as both a necessary state for self-knowledge and a source of existential anguish

memes.label: Sophroniska
memes.description: A Polish psychoanalyst at the Saas-Fee sanatorium who treats Boris through confession-based methods — representing the modern, scientific approach to the human psyche in contrast with the religious and moral frameworks of other characters

memes.label: The Sorbonne
memes.description: The historic university in the Latin Quarter of Paris where Olivier attends lectures and where the intellectual atmosphere of youthful ambition, literary rivalry, and the novel's portrait of academic life is centered

memes.label: Strouvilhou
memes.description: Shadowy mastermind of the counterfeit coin ring, cousin of Léon Ghéridanisol, who uses schoolboys to circulate false coins and represents the criminal underworld that parallels the "respectable" counterfeit of bourgeois society

memes.label: Tag Index
memes.description: Browse all wiki entries by tag — grouped views of characters, concepts, events, and places organized by thematic tags.

memes.label: The Argonauts
memes.description: A Parisian literary circle and review that serves as the established rival to Passavant's Vanguard — hosts of the banquet scene where literary careerism and the circulation of counterfeit reputation are on full display

memes.label: "The Banquet Scene"
memes.description: The literary banquet organized by Robert de Passavant's circle — a set-piece of social performance that brings together the novel's competing worlds of art, ambition, and moral compromise, serving as the institutional form of the counterfeit coin's circulation of false value

memes.label: "The Counterfeit Coin Discussion"
memes.description: "At Saas-Fée, Edouard, Bernard, and Sophroniska debate the counterfeit coin as a literary method and the relationship between facts and ideas in fiction"

memes.label: The Fatal Pistol
memes.description: "The tragic suicide of young Boris at the Pension Vedel-Azaïs, orchestrated by a cruel game among the schoolboys and culminating in the fatal discharge of La Pérouse's pistol — the novel's most devastating event"

memes.label: The Swiss Sanatorium
memes.description: The health resort at Saas-Fée where Edouard, Bernard, Laura, Sophroniska, Boris, and Bronja gather in Part II — a space of convalescence, confession, and the novel's most concentrated exploration of childhood, psychoanalysis, and moral reflection

memes.label: "The Schoolboy Counterfeit Coin Ring"
memes.description: "George Molinier and his schoolboy friends pass counterfeit coins at a tobacco shop, drawing the attention of the authorities"

memes.label: The Seine
memes.description: The river that runs through Paris, serving in The Counterfeiters as the scene of Vincent's solitary walks, moments of moral reckoning, and the symbolic geography of the city's divided self

memes.label: Transition
memes.description: The process of personal transformation — moral, intellectual, and emotional — that every major character in The Counterfeiters undergoes, often catalyzed by crisis, discovery, or departure from familiar surroundings

memes.label: Tuileries Gardens
memes.description: The formal gardens west of the Louvre where Vincent walks after his night with Lilian, pressing a wet handkerchief to his forehead — a site of postlapsarian wandering and the novel's image of moral disorientation

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: Self-regarding pride and the desire for admiration as recurrent psychological drivers of conduct in The Counterfeiters

memes.label: The Vedel Boarding School
memes.description: The Protestant boarding school near the Luxembourg Gardens run by the Vedel-Azaïs family, where Armand, Sarah, and the younger generation of characters live and study

memes.label: Vincent Molinier
memes.description: Olivier's older brother who abandons his pregnant mistress Laura for Lady Griffith — the novel's primary example of rationalized moral failure

memes.label: "Vincent's Night with Lilian"
memes.description: "Vincent spends the night with Lady Griffith at Passavant's apartment while 'the devil casts up his accounts'"

memes.label: Works Referenced
memes.description: Literary and artistic works referenced in André Gide's The Counterfeiters, including books, plays, and poems mentioned by characters or the narrator.
